    13.4 Utility Function (Fn’ü»’ü»’ü») Operations on the Panel Operator
                Utility functions are used to set up and tune the SERVOPACK.
                The Panel Operator displays numbers beginning with ŌĆ£Fn.ŌĆØ
                Display Example: Origin Search



                The operating procedures from the Panel Operator are described here. Refer to the descrip-
                tions of individual utility functions for preparations and related parameters.
                Refer to the following section for details on utility functions.
                       15.2.1 Corresponding SERVOPACK Utility Function Names on page 15-7




    13.4.1      Display Alarm History (Fn000)
                Refer to the following section for information on this utility function other than the procedure.
                       12.2.4 Displaying the Alarm History on page 12-43

                            Panel Display after
                 Step                                           Keys                                 Operation
                               Operation


                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                               Press the MODE/SET Key to enter Utility Function
                   1                                                           Mode. If Fn000 is not displayed, press the UP Key or
                                                                               DOWN Key to display Fn000.
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/


                                                                               Press the DATA/SHIFT Key for approximately one sec-
                   2
                                                                               ond. The most recent alarm will be displayed.
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/

                                                                               Press the DOWN Key to display the next older alarm.
                                                                               Press the UP Key to display the next newer alarm. The
                                                                               higher the far-left segment is, the older the alarm is.
                   3
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                               Refer to the following section for information on
                                    Alarm number                               alarms.
                                                                                   12.2.1 List of Alarms on page 12-5

                                                                               Press the DATA/SHIFT Key. The lower four digits of
                   4
                                                                               the time stamp will be displayed.
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/


                                                                               Press the DATA/SHIFT Key. The middle four digits of
                   5
                                                                               the time stamp will be displayed.
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/


                                                                               Press the DATA/SHIFT Key. The upper two digits of
                   6
                                                                               the time stamp will be displayed.
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/


                                                                               Press the DATA/SHIFT Key. The alarm number will
                   7
                                                                               flash on the display.
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/


                                                                               Press the DATA/SHIFT Key again for approximately
                   8
                                                                               one second to return the display to Fn000.
                                                     MODE/SET          DATA/




                             Time Stamp
                             A time stamp gives the total operation time to the point at which the alarm occurred in incre-
                             ments of 100 ms from when the control power supply and main circuit power supply were turned
                   Term      ON. For 24-hour, 365-day operation, measurements are possible for approximately 13 years.



                   Example       Time Stamp Display Example
                                 If 36000 is displayed, 3,600,000 ms = 3,600 s = 60 min = 1 h
                                 Therefore, the total operation time in hours is 1 hour.

    13.4.14 Manually Adjust Motor Current Detection Signal Offset (Fn00F)



    13.4.14 Manually Adjust Motor Current Detection Signal Offset
            (Fn00F)
                Refer to the following section for information on this utility function other than the procedure.
                       6.16.2 Manual Adjustment on page 6-105

                           Panel Display after
                 Step                                          Keys                                  Operation
                              Operation

                                                                              Press the MODE/SET Key to enter Utility Function
                   1
                                                                              Mode.
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/


                   2                                                          Press the UP Key or DOWN Key to display Fn00F.
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/

                                                                              First you adjust the offset for phase U (Cu1-o). Press
                   3                                                          the DATA/SHIFT Key for approximately one second.
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/   The display shown at the left will appear.

                                                                              Press the DATA/SHIFT Key. The amount of offset for
                   4
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                              phase U will be displayed.

                                                                              Press the UP Key or DOWN Key to change the
                                                                              amount of offset. Change the setting by approximately
                                                                              10 units at a time in the direction that reduces the
                   5
                                                                              torque ripple to find the value that minimizes the torque
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                              ripple, and set that value.
                                                                              Adjustment range: -512 to +511

                                                                              Press the DATA/SHIFT Key. The display shown on the
                   6
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                              left will appear again.

                                                                              Next you adjust the offset for phase V (Cu2-o). Press
                   7                                                          the MODE/SET Key for approximately one second.
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/   The display shown at the left will appear.

                                                                              Press the DATA/SHIFT Key. The amount of offset for
                   8
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                              phase V will be displayed.

                                                                              Press the UP Key or DOWN Key to change the
                                                                              amount of offset. In the same way as for the phase-U
                                                                              adjustment, change the setting by approximately 10
                   9                                                          units at a time in the direction that reduces the torque
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/   ripple to find the value that minimizes the torque ripple,
                                                                              and set that value.
                                                                              Adjustment range: -512 to +511
                                                                              Press the DATA/SHIFT Key for approximately one sec-
                  10                                                          ond. Cu2-o will be displayed, and then Fn00F will be
                                                    MODE/SET          DATA/   displayed again.
                          Reduce the amount by which you change the offsets each time and repeat steps 3 to 10* to fine-tune
                  11
                          the amounts of offset.
                * Examples of the Amount of Change to Adjust Offsets
                    ŌĆó First time: 10 units at a time
                    ŌĆó Second time: 5 units at a time
                    ŌĆó Third time: 1 unit at a time
                  The above values are guidelines. Vary the amount of change and the number of repetitions according to your sys-
                  tem.

                    3                                                                            the load moment of inertia exceeds the allowable
                                                            MODE/SET             DATA/           level (i.e., outside the scope of product warranty),
                                                                                                 or if the mass ratio is 30 or higher (i.e., outside the

                                                                                                 change the load level to 2.


                                                                                           Press the MODE/SET Key to display. The rigidity
                    4                                                                      level setting display for the tuning-less levels will
                                                            MODE/SET             DATA/     appear.


                                                                                           Press the UP Key or DOWN Key to set the rigidity
                                                                                           level. Set the rigidity level to between 0 and 7. The
                                                                                           larger the value is, the higher the gain is and the
                                                                                           better response performance will be. (The default
                    5                                                                      setting is 4.)
                                                                                           Note: Vibration may occur if the rigidity level is too high.
                                           Rigidity level   MODE/SET             DATA/           Lower the rigidity level if vibration occurs. If high-
                                                                                                 frequency noise is generated, press the DATA/
                                                                                                 SHIFT Key to automatically set a notch filter for
                                                                                                 the vibration frequency.

                    7

                                                                   Press the UP Key or DOWN Key to enter Tuning
                                                                   Mode.
                                                                   Tuning Mode (Strength of Tuning Setting)
         4
                                       MODE/SET           DATA/
                                                                   0: Performs tuning giving priority to stability.
                                                                   1: Performs tuning giving priority to response.
                                                                   Note: The rigidity type is always 2.

                                                                   If the servo is OFF (i.e., if power is not supplied to the




                                                                                                                                     Panel Displays and Panel Operator Procedures
                                                                   Servomotor), input the /S-ON (Servo ON) from the
         5                                         ŌĆō
                                                                   host controller.

         6                                                         ond. The one-parameter gain will be displayed as
                                       MODE/SET           DATA/    shown on the left.

                                                                   parameter gain value and change the actual servo
                                                                   gains (Pn100, Pn101, Pn102, and Pn401) at the
         7
                                                                   same time.
                                       MODE/SET           DATA/
                                                                   You end this tuning function when you decide that
                                                                   the response is satisfactory.
                                                                   Press the MODE/SET Key to save the four calcu-
                                                                   lated gains to the parameters. When tuning has been
         8                                                         completed, donE will flash on the display, and then
                                      MODE/SET            DATA/    the display shown on the left will appear again.
                                                                   Note: To end operation without saving the calculated gains,
                                                                         go to step 9.

         9
                                       MODE/SET           DATA/
                                                                   second to return the display to Fn203.

                   1

                   3
                                                      MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                                Panel Operator will enter Reference Amplitude Setting
                           (Reference amplitude                                 Mode.
                                  setting)
                                                                                Press the UP Key or DOWN Key to set the reference
                                                                                amplitude. Reference amplitude setting range: 1 to 800
                                                                                Note: 1. If you are setting Easy FFT for the first time, do not
                                                                                         change the reference amplitude setting, but rather
                   4                                                                     start from the default value of 15. Although increasing
                                                      MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                                         the reference amplitude will increase the detection
                                                                                         accuracy, the vibration and noise in the machine will
                                                                                         increase momentarily. Increase the reference ampli-
                                                                                         tude a little at a time and observe the results.
                                                                                      2. The set reference amplitude will be stored in Pn456.


                                                                                Press the DATA/SHIFT Key for approximately one sec-
                   5
                                                      MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                                ond. The ready to operate status will be entered.
                          (Ready to operate sta-
                                   tus)
                                                                                Press the MODE/SET Key to turn ON the servo.
                                                                                Additional Information
                   6
                                                                                Press the MODE/SET Key again to turn OFF the servo.
                                                      MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                                                Return to step 5.
                                                                                While the servo is ON, press the UP Key (forward) or
                                                                                the DOWN Key (reverse). The Servomotor will auto-
                                                                                matically perform round-trip operation, moving in for-
                                                                                ward and reverse several times for 1/4th of a rotation
                                 (Flashing)                                     for a Rotary Servomotor and 10 mm or less for a Linear
                                                                                Servomotor. The Servomotor performs this operation
                   7                                                            for approximately 2 seconds. During this operation, the
                                                      MODE/SET          DATA/   display shown on the left will flash.
                                                                                Note: 1. Press the MODE/SET Key to cancel the operation.
                                                                                         Return to step 5.
                                                                                      2. The Servomotor will move slightly. Also at the same
                            Slight movement in                                           time, the Servomotor will emit noise. To ensure
                                                                                         safety, do not approach or enter the range of
                                Servomotor                                               machine motion.

        Panel Display after
Step                                       Keys                                     Operation
           Operation
                                                          If detection is completed normally, E_FFt will stop
                                                          flashing and the detected resonance frequency will be
                                                          displayed. If detection fails, F---- will be displayed.
                                                          To check the results, go to step 9. To monitor the reso-
                                                          nance frequency without setting the detection result,
                                                          press the DATA/SHIFT Key for approximately one sec-
                                                          ond and return to step 2.
                                                          ’ü« IMPORTANT
 8                                          ŌĆō             If the operation ended normally but it took two sec-
       (Detection result dis-                             onds or longer, the detection accuracy may not be suf-
          play example)                                   ficient. Increase the reference amplitude to a value
                                                          higher than 15 to increase the detection accuracy.
                                                          Although increasing the reference amplitude will
                                                          increase the detection accuracy, the vibration and
                                                          noise in the machine will increase momentarily.
                                                          Increase the reference amplitude a little at a time and
                                                          observe the results.
                                                          Press the MODE/SET Key. The optimum notch filter
                                                          for the detected resonance frequency will automatically
                                                          be set. If the notch filter is set correctly, donE will flash
                                                          on the display, and then the display shown on the left
                                                          will appear.
                                                          If the 1st notch filter frequency is already set (Pn408 =
 9                                                        n.’ü»’ü»’ü»1), the 2nd notch filter frequency will be auto-
                                MODE/SET          DATA/   matically set (Pn40C).
                                                          Press the MODE/SET Key to return to step 5.
                                                          Note: 1. If both the 1st and 2nd notch filter frequencies are
                                                                   already set (Pn408 = n.’ü»1’ü»1), no more notch filter
                                                                   frequencies can be set.
                                                                2. If the frequency detected by this function is not to be
                                                                   used, set Pn408 to n.’ü»’ü»’ü»0 (Disable notch filter).

                                                          Press the DATA/SHIFT Key for approximately one sec-
10
                                MODE/SET          DATA/
                                                          ond to return the display to Fn206.

       14.1.1      Interpreting the Parameter Lists
                                 The types of Servomotors to which the parameter applies.
                                   All: The parameter is used for both Rotary Servomotors and Linear Servomotors.
                                   Rotary: The parameter is used for only Rotary Servomotors.
                                   Linear: The parameter is used for only Linear Servomotors.
                                 Rotary Servomotor terms are used for parameters that are applicable
                                 to all Servomotors. If you are using a Linear Servomotor, you need to
                                 interpret the terms accordingly. Refer to the following section for
                                 details.                                                                               Indicates when a change to the
                                          ’üĄ Differences in Terms for Rotary Servomotors and                             parameter will be effective.

Pn628*2                Parameters


*1. Set a percentage of the motor rated torque.
*2. These parameters are for SERVOPACKs with a Safety Module. Refer to the following manual for details.
          ╬Ż-V-Series/╬Ż-V-Series for Large-Capacity Models/╬Ż-7-Series UserŌĆÖs Manual Safety Module (Manual No.: SIEP
          C720829 06)
*3. Normally set this parameter to 0. If you use an External Regenerative Resistor, set the capacity (W) of the Exter-
     nal Regenerative Resistor.
*4. The upper limit is the maximum output capacity (W) of the SERVOPACK.
*5. These parameters are for SERVOPACKs with the dynamic brake option. Refer to the following manual for details.
          ╬Ż-7-Series AC Servo Drive ╬Ż-7S/╬Ż-7W SERVOPACK with Dynamic Brake Hardware Option Specifications Prod-
          uct Manual (Manual No.: SIEP S800001 73)
*6. The SGLFW2 is the only Yaskawa Linear Servomotor that supports this function.
*7. Enabled only when Pn61A is set to n.’ü»’ü»’ü»2 or n.’ü»’ü»’ü»3.

                   Note: 1. Cables to connect the SERVOPACK to the MP2000/MP3000 are available from Yaskawa.
                            For details, refer to the manual for the Machine Controller.
                         2. Only signals that are applicable to the MP2000/MP3000-Series SVA-01 Motion Module and the SERVO-
                            PACK are shown in the diagram.
                         3. The main circuit power supply for the SERVOPACK in this connection example is three-phase 200 VAC.
                         4. Incorrect wiring may damage the Machine Controller or SERVOPACK. Wire all connections carefully.
                         5. Do not wire any unused signal lines (i.e., leave them open).
                         6. The above wiring diagram shows the connections for only one axis. If you will use other axes, make con-
                            nections to the SERVOPACK in the same way.
                         7. All normally closed input terminals that are not used at the Machine ControllerŌĆÖs I/O connector section must
                            be connected at the connector.
                         8. Set the parameters so that the servo can be turned ON and OFF with the /S-ON (Servo ON) signal.

              15.1.2 Example of Connections to Yokogawa ElectricŌĆÖs F3YP2’ü»-0P Positioning Module for Position Control

               9. The SERVOPACK provides safety functions to protect people from the hazardous operation of the moving
                  parts of the machine. In order to use the safety functions, the required circuits must be configured for CN8.
                  If the safety functions will not be used, leave the enclosed Safety Jumper Connector connected to the
                  SERVOPACK (CN8). Refer to the following chapter for details.
                        Chapter 11 Safety Functions

                       Immediate stop input A20

                                                         FG

         *1. The ALM (Servo Alarm) signal is output for approximately five seconds when the power supply is turned ON.
             Take this into consideration when designing the power ON sequence. Also, use the ALM signal to actuate the
             alarm detection relay (1Ry) to stop the main circuit power supply to the SERVOPACK.
         *2. Set Pn200 to n.’ü»’ü»’ü»1 (CW and CCW pulse trains with positive logic).
         *3. Connect the shielded wire to the connector shell.

         *4.           represents a shielded twisted-pair cable.
         Note: 1. Only the signals that are applicable to the SERVOPACK and the OMRON Position Control Unit are shown
                  in the diagram.
               2. The main circuit power supply for the SERVOPACK in this connection example is three-phase 200 VAC.
               3. Incorrect wiring may damage the Position Control Unit or SERVOPACK. Wire all connections carefully.
               4. Do not wire any unused signal lines (i.e., leave them open).
               5. The above wiring diagram shows the connections for only the X axis. If you will use other axes, make con-
                  nections to the SERVOPACK in the same way.
               6. All normally closed input terminals that are not used at the Position Control UnitŌĆÖs I/O connector section
                  must be connected at the connector.
               7. Set the parameters so that the servo can be turned ON and OFF with the /S-ON (Servo ON) signal.
               8. The SERVOPACK provides safety functions to protect people from the hazardous operation of the moving
                  parts of the machine. In order to use the safety functions, the required circuits must be configured for CN8.
                  If the safety functions will not be used, leave the enclosed Safety Jumper Connector connected to the
                  SERVOPACK (CN8). Refer to the following chapter for details.
                        Chapter 11 Safety Functions
